items.title: The Constitution 131st Amendment Bill 2026
items.whyInNews: The Union government has introduced the Constitution 131st Amendment Bill 2026, the Delimitation Bill 2026, and the Union Territories Laws Amendment Bill 2026, to enable fresh delimitation based on the latest available Census, expand the Lok Sabha, and operationalise 33% women’s reservation in legislatures, as of Saturday, 18 April 2026.
items.summary: The government has introduced three Bills to enable fresh delimitation using recent Census data, expand the Lok Sabha to 850 seats, and implement 33% reservation for women, aiming to uphold the principle of “one person, one vote, one value”, with a new Delimitation Commission to redraw constituencies and allocate seats, and the move is expected to have an impact on federalism and representation of southern states.
keyPoints: Expansion of Lok Sabha to 850 seats
Fresh delimitation based on2011  Census data
New Delimitation Commission to redraw constituencies
importantPoints: One person, one vote, one value principle
Impact on federalism and southern states
Increase in Lok Sabha seats from 550 to 850
Delimitation Commission decisions to be final and legally binding
items.examRelevance: This is relevant to the Polity and Governance section of the SSC exams, as it deals with Constitutional amendments, delimitation, and women’s reservation, which are important topics for the General Awareness and Current Affairs sections.
